title: Your First Python Program
summary: Write and run your first Python program: Hello, World!

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to Python
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this lesson, you will learn how to write and execute your first Python program, which will print 'Hello, World!' to the console.

quizDraft.title: Write Your First Program
quizDraft.prompt: Create a Python script that prints 'Hello, World!'

help.concept: The print function in Python outputs text to the console.
help.hint_1: Remember to use parentheses with the print function.
help.hint_2: Make sure to include quotes around the text.

quizDraft.title: Modify Your Program
quizDraft.prompt: Change your program to print 'Hello, [Your Name]!'
quizDraft.hint: You can replace 'World' with your name.

help.concept: You can customize the output of the print function by changing the string inside it.
help.hint_1: Make sure to keep the print function intact.
help.hint_2: Use your actual name in the string.

quizDraft.title: Add a New Line
quizDraft.prompt: Modify your program to print 'Hello, [Your Name]!' on one line and 'Welcome to Python!' on the next line.

help.concept: In Python, you can use '\n' to create a new line in the output.
help.hint_1: Make sure to include both print statements.
help.hint_2: Combine the strings with '\n' between them.

quizDraft.title: Identify the Output
quizDraft.prompt: What will the following code output? `print('Hello, World!')` The output will be: `Hello, ____!`
quizDraft.hint: Think about what the print function does.

help.concept: The print function outputs the string exactly as it is written.
help.hint_1: Focus on the text inside the quotes.
help.hint_2: Remember that 'World' is part of the output.

quizDraft.title: Understanding Print Function
quizDraft.prompt: What does the print function do in Python?
quizDraft.hint: It outputs text to the console.

help.concept: The print function is used to display information to the user.
help.hint_1: It can print strings, numbers, and more.
help.hint_2: Think about where you see the output when you run a program.

options.text: It outputs text to the console.
It reads input from the user.
